topics.title: Public Provident Fund (PPF) Complete Guide
topics.answer:
**Public Provident Fund (PPF)** is India's most popular long-term savings scheme backed by the Government.

**Key Features 2025:**
• Interest Rate: 7.1% p.a. (compounded annually)
• Min Investment: ₹500/year
• Max Investment: ₹1,50,000/year
• Lock-in Period: 15 years (extendable in 5-year blocks)
• Tax Benefits: EEE (Exempt-Exempt-Exempt)
• Account Opening: Any bank or post office

**Maturity Calculation:**
₹1.5 lakh/year × 15 years at 7.1% = ₹40.68 lakhs

**Withdrawal Rules:**
• Partial withdrawal: From 7th year (50% of balance)
• Loan facility: 3rd to 6th year
• Premature closure: Only for medical emergency

**Tax Benefits:**
• Section 80C deduction: Up to ₹1.5L
• Interest earned: 100% tax-free
• Maturity amount: 100% tax-free

**Best For:** Risk-averse investors seeking guaranteed returns

**Calculate:** [PPF Calculator](https://moneycal.in/calculators/ppf-calculator)
**Source:** NSI India (nsiindia.gov.in)

topics.title: SIP (Systematic Investment Plan) Complete Guide
topics.answer:
**SIP (Systematic Investment Plan)** - Invest fixed amounts regularly in mutual funds.

**How SIP Works:**
• Choose mutual fund scheme
• Set monthly investment (₹500 minimum)
• Auto-debit from bank account
• Get units at current NAV
• Build wealth through rupee cost averaging

**Historical Returns:**
• Large Cap Funds: 10-12% p.a.
• Mid Cap Funds: 12-15% p.a.
• Small Cap Funds: 15-18% p.a.
• ELSS (Tax Saving): 12-15% p.a.

**Example Calculation:**
₹10,000/month for 20 years at 12% = ₹99.91 lakhs
(Investment: ₹24L, Returns: ₹75.91L)

**Tax Treatment:**
• LTCG: 10% above ₹1 lakh/year
• STCG: 15% (if sold < 1 year)
• ELSS: 3-year lock-in, 80C benefit

**SIP Benefits:**
✅ Disciplined investing
✅ Rupee cost averaging
✅ Power of compounding
✅ Flexibility (pause/stop anytime)
✅ Low minimum (₹500)

**Best SIP Funds 2025:**
• Axis Bluechip Fund
• Mirae Asset Large Cap
• Parag Parikh Flexi Cap
• SBI Small Cap Fund

**Calculate:** [SIP Calculator](https://moneycal.in/calculators/sip-calculator)
**Start SIP:** Any mutual fund platform (Groww, Zerodha, Paytm Money)

topics.title: GST (Goods and Services Tax) Complete Guide India 2025
topics.answer:
**GST (Goods & Services Tax)** - India's unified indirect tax system.

**GST Rates (5 Slabs):**
• 0%: Essential goods (vegetables, milk, grains)
• 5%: Processed foods, transport
• 12%: Computers, butter
• 18%: Most goods & services (default)
• 28%: Luxury items, sin goods

**GST Returns & Deadlines:**
• GSTR-1 (Sales): 11th of next month
• GSTR-3B (Summary + Payment): 20th of next month
• GSTR-9 (Annual): 31st December

**Registration Threshold:**
• Goods: ₹40 lakhs turnover
• Services: ₹20 lakhs turnover
• Special states: ₹10/20 lakhs

**HSN/SAC Codes:**
• Turnover > ₹5 Cr: 6-digit mandatory
• ₹1.5-5 Cr: 4-digit mandatory
• < ₹1.5 Cr: Optional

**Input Tax Credit (ITC):**
• Claim GST paid on purchases
• Reduce GST liability on sales
• Conditions: Valid invoice, business use, supplier paid tax

**Late Filing Penalty:**
• ₹100/day (₹50 CGST + ₹50 SGST)
• Max: ₹5,000

**Our GST Tools:**
• [HSN/SAC Finder](https://moneycal.in/tools/gst-hsn-sac-finder)
• [GSTR-1 Calculator](https://moneycal.in/tools/gstr-1-deadline-calculator)
• [GST Calculator](https://moneycal.in/tools/gst-amount-calculator)
• [ITC Checker](https://moneycal.in/tools/itc-eligibility-checker)

**Official Portal:** gst.gov.in

topics.title: Income Tax India FY 2024-25 (AY 2025-26) Complete Guide
topics.answer:
**Income Tax India - FY 2024-25 Guide:**

**New Tax Regime (Default):**
• ₹0-3L: Nil
• ₹3-6L: 5%
• ₹6-9L: 10%
• ₹9-12L: 15%
• ₹12-15L: 20%
• Above ₹15L: 30%

**Rebate u/s 87A:** Income up to ₹7 lakh = ZERO tax!

**Old Tax Regime:**
• ₹0-2.5L: Nil
• ₹2.5-5L: 5%
• ₹5-10L: 20%
• Above ₹10L: 30%
• Allows deductions (80C, 80D, HRA)

**Major Deductions (Old Regime):**
• 80C: ₹1.5L (PPF, ELSS, Insurance, etc.)
• 80D: ₹25K-₹1L (Health insurance)
• 80CCD(1B): ₹50K (Additional NPS)
• HRA: Rent paid exemption
• 80E: Education loan interest
• 24(b): ₹2L (Home loan interest)

**Which Regime to Choose?**
• New: If salary < ₹10L or minimal deductions
• Old: If investments in 80C, HRA, home loan

**ITR Filing Deadlines:**
• Individuals: 31st July
• Audit cases: 31st October
• Revised return: 31st December

**Advance Tax (If liability > ₹10K):**
• 15% by 15th June
• 45% by 15th September
• 75% by 15th December
• 100% by 15th March

**Calculate Tax:**
• [Income Tax Calculator](https://moneycal.in/calculators/income-tax-calculator)
• [Section 80C Calculator](https://moneycal.in/calculators/section-80c-calculator)
• [HRA Calculator](https://moneycal.in/calculators/hra-calculator)

**Official:** incometax.gov.in

topics.title: EMI (Equated Monthly Installment) Complete Guide
topics.answer:
**EMI (Equated Monthly Installment)** - Fixed monthly payment for loans.

**EMI Formula:**
EMI = [P × R × (1+R)^N] / [(1+R)^N - 1]

Where:
• P = Principal (loan amount)
• R = Monthly interest rate (Annual/12/100)
• N = Tenure in months

**Example: ₹10 Lakh Home Loan**
• Loan: ₹10,00,000
• Rate: 9% p.a. (0.75% monthly)
• Tenure: 20 years (240 months)
• **EMI: ₹8,997/month**
• Total Payment: ₹21,59,280
• Total Interest: ₹11,59,280

**Current Interest Rates (Oct 2024):**
**Home Loans:**
• SBI: 8.50-9.65%
• HDFC: 8.60-9.50%
• ICICI: 8.75-9.50%

**Personal Loans:**
• 10.50-24% (based on credit score)

**Car Loans:**
• 8.50-12%

**EMI Components:**
• First EMI: Mostly interest
• Last EMI: Mostly principal
• Interest portion decreases over time
• Principal portion increases

**How to Reduce EMI:**
✅ Make prepayments
✅ Choose shorter tenure
✅ Negotiate lower interest rate
✅ Improve credit score

**Tax Benefits:**
• Home Loan: ₹1.5L (principal) + ₹2L (interest)
• No tax benefits for car/personal loans

**Calculate EMI:**
• [EMI Calculator](https://moneycal.in/calculators/emi-calculator)
• [Home Loan Calculator](https://moneycal.in/calculators/home-loan-calculator)
• [Car Loan Calculator](https://moneycal.in/calculators/car-loan-calculator)
• [Prepayment Calculator](https://moneycal.in/calculators/loan-prepayment-calculator)

quickAnswers.What is PPF?: PPF is a 15-year government savings scheme offering 7.1% interest with complete tax exemption. Max investment: ₹1.5L/year.
quickAnswers.How to calculate SIP returns?: Use our SIP Calculator. Example: ₹10K/month for 10 years at 12% = ₹23.23 lakhs (Investment: ₹12L).
quickAnswers.GST filing deadline?: GSTR-1: 11th, GSTR-3B: 20th of next month. Late fee: ₹100/day (max ₹5,000).
quickAnswers.Income tax for ₹10 lakhs?: New Regime: ₹1,12,500. Old Regime: Varies with deductions. Use our Income Tax Calculator.
quickAnswers.Best investment for 2025?: Diversify: 40% Mutual Funds (SIP), 30% PPF, 20% NPS, 10% FD. Adjust based on risk appetite.
quickAnswers.How to save tax?: Section 80C (₹1.5L): PPF, ELSS, Insurance. 80D (₹25K): Health insurance. 80CCD1B (₹50K): NPS. Total savings: ₹92K in 30% bracket!
quickAnswers.What is CAGR?: Compound Annual Growth Rate - smoothed annualized return. Formula: [(Final/Initial)^(1/Years)] - 1. Better than absolute returns for comparison.
quickAnswers.NPS vs PPF which is better?: PPF: 7.1% guaranteed, tax-free. NPS: 10-14% market-linked, partial tax. Use both! 50-50 ideal for balanced retirement.
quickAnswers.How to file GST return?: Step 1: File GSTR-1 (sales) by 11th. Step 2: File GSTR-3B (summary) by 20th. Step 3: Pay tax via challan. Use our GSTR-1 Calculator.
quickAnswers.What is HSN code?: 6-8 digit code to classify goods under GST. Example: 8517 = Mobile phones. Mandatory based on turnover. Find codes: HSN/SAC Finder.
quickAnswers.Home loan tax benefits?: Principal: ₹1.5L under 80C. Interest: ₹2L under 24(b). First home (₹45L): Extra ₹50K under 80EEA. Total: ₹4L deduction!
quickAnswers.What is compound interest?: Interest on interest. Money grows exponentially. Formula: A = P(1+r/n)^(nt). Example: ₹1L at 8% for 10 years = ₹2.16L.
quickAnswers.FD vs PPF which is better?: FD: 6.5-7.25%, interest taxable. PPF: 7.1%, completely tax-free. PPF wins for tax-free returns! But FD offers liquidity.
quickAnswers.How to calculate retirement corpus?: Need 25-30X annual expenses. Example: ₹50K/month = ₹1.5-1.8 crore corpus. Start SIP early! Use Retirement Planner.
quickAnswers.What is XIRR?: Extended Internal Rate of Return - for irregular cash flows (SIPs with different amounts). Better than CAGR for SIP. Use XIRR Calculator.
quickAnswers.Section 80C investments?: PPF, ELSS, Life Insurance, NSC, Tax-saving FD, Home loan principal, Tuition fees, SSY. Max: ₹1.5 lakh total.
quickAnswers.How to claim HRA?: Minimum of: Actual HRA, 50%/40% salary (metro/non-metro), Rent - 10% salary. Calculate: HRA Calculator.
quickAnswers.Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana?: Girl child (< 10 years) savings. Rate: 8.0% p.a. Max: ₹1.5L/year. Maturity: 21 years. Tax: EEE. Best for daughters!
quickAnswers.NPS Tier 1 vs Tier 2?: Tier 1: Retirement account, locked till 60, tax benefits. Tier 2: Like mutual fund, withdraw anytime, no tax benefit. Both needed!
quickAnswers.Mutual fund exit load?: Fee charged if sold early. Usually 1% if < 1 year. ELSS: 3-year lock-in (no exit before). Check scheme doc.
quickAnswers.What is NAV in mutual funds?: Net Asset Value - price per unit. Updated daily. Buy/sell at NAV. Example: NAV ₹100, invest ₹10K = 100 units.
quickAnswers.ELSS vs PPF?: ELSS: 12-15% returns, 3-year lock, market risk. PPF: 7.1% guaranteed, 15-year lock, zero risk. ELSS for growth, PPF for safety!
quickAnswers.How to open PPF account online?: SBI/HDFC/ICICI net banking → Accounts → Open PPF. Or Post Office iPPF app. KYC needed: PAN, Aadhaar, photo.
quickAnswers.Gold investment options?: Physical gold, Gold ETF, Sovereign Gold Bonds, Digital gold. SGBs best: 2.5% interest + price appreciation + tax-free!
quickAnswers.What is advance tax?: Pay tax quarterly if liability > ₹10K. Due: 15 Jun, 15 Sep, 15 Dec, 15 Mar. Calculate: Advance Tax Calculator.
quickAnswers.TDS on salary?: Employer deducts tax based on declaration (Form 12BB). Submit proof (80C, HRA, etc.) to reduce TDS. Check Form 26AS.
quickAnswers.ITR filing for salary?: ITR-1 (Sahaj) for salary < ₹50L, one house. ITR-2 for capital gains, multiple properties. Deadline: 31 July.
quickAnswers.Can I have 2 PPF accounts?: No! Only ONE per person (post-2019 rule). But can open for spouse + 2 kids = Total family ₹6-7.5L investment.
quickAnswers.Best tax saving options?: 80C: PPF/ELSS (₹1.5L). 80D: Health insurance (₹25K). 80CCD1B: NPS (₹50K). Total: ₹2.25L deductions = ₹92K tax saved!
quickAnswers.How to calculate CAGR?: CAGR = [(Final/Initial)^(1/Years)] - 1 × 100. Example: ₹1L → ₹1.6L in 3 years = 16.96% CAGR. Use CAGR Calculator.
quickAnswers.What is XIRR vs CAGR?: CAGR: Lump sum investments. XIRR: Multiple cash flows (SIPs). XIRR more accurate for SIP returns.
quickAnswers.Car loan vs personal loan?: Car Loan: 8-12%, secured by car, longer tenure. Personal: 11-24%, unsecured, faster approval. Car loan cheaper!
quickAnswers.Home loan prepayment benefit?: ₹50L loan, 9%, 20Y. Prepay ₹1L in Year 1 → Save ₹1.9L interest! Use Prepayment Calculator.
quickAnswers.Credit card EMI vs personal loan?: CC EMI: 12-18%, instant, small amounts. Personal Loan: 11-16%, higher amounts, lower rate. Personal loan better for large purchases.
quickAnswers.What is gratuity?: Employer pays 4.81% of basic salary. Formula: (Last salary × Years) / 26 × 15. Max: ₹20 lakhs. Tax-free! Calculate: Gratuity Calculator.
quickAnswers.NPS withdrawal rules?: At 60: Take 60% lump sum, 40% mandatory annuity. Or withdraw 100% if corpus < ₹5L. Partial withdrawal: 25% for specific needs.
quickAnswers.Mutual fund overlap checker?: Checks if multiple funds hold same stocks. High overlap = concentrated risk. Use: Mutual Fund Overlap Checker tool.
quickAnswers.What is SWP?: Systematic Withdrawal Plan - withdraw fixed amount monthly from mutual fund. Tax-efficient for retirement income. Opposite of SIP!
quickAnswers.Gold ETF vs Physical gold?: Gold ETF: No making charges, easy to sell, can do SIP. Physical: Sentiment value, locker needed. SGB best: 2.5% interest!
quickAnswers.How to check credit score free?: CIBIL: Free once/year. Experian, Equifax: Free monthly on apps (Paytm, PhonePe). Good score: 750+, Excellent: 800+.
quickAnswers.LIC vs mutual fund?: LIC: Insurance + Investment (low returns 5-6%). Mutual Fund: Pure investment (12-15%). Recommendation: Term insurance + Mutual fund SIP.
quickAnswers.What is DRE in stocks?: Dividend Reinvestment - dividends auto-invested to buy more shares. Compounding effect. Not common in India (opt-in).
quickAnswers.How to invest in US stocks from India?: Via apps: Groww, Vested, Ind Money. LRS limit: $250K/year. Tax: 25% US + India slab. Currency risk: Consider!
quickAnswers.Crypto tax in India?: 30% flat on profits + 1% TDS. No loss offset. No deductions. Gifts also taxed. Very high! Use: Crypto Tax Calculator.
quickAnswers.What is tax harvesting?: Sell investments to realize ₹1L LTCG (tax-free limit), reinvest immediately. Resets cost price, saves future tax!
quickAnswers.Senior citizen FD rates?: Extra 0.50% than regular. Best: SBI/Post Office (7.5%). No TDS if income < ₹5L (Form 15H).
quickAnswers.What is debt mutual fund?: Invests in bonds, govt securities. Low risk. Returns: 6-9%. Tax: LTCG 20% with indexation (if > 3 years). Good for conservative investors.
quickAnswers.Index fund vs active fund?: Index: Tracks Nifty/Sensex, low cost (0.1-0.5%), passive. Active: Fund manager picks stocks, high cost (1-2%), aims to beat market. Index wins long-term!
quickAnswers.What is STP in mutual funds?: Systematic Transfer Plan - transfer fixed amount from one fund to another monthly. Use: Move from debt to equity gradually.
quickAnswers.EPF vs PPF difference?: EPF: Employer mandatory, 8.25% interest, locked till 58. PPF: Voluntary, 7.1%, locked 15 years. Both tax-free!
quickAnswers.How to withdraw PF online?: UAN portal → Online Services → Claim (Form-31, 19, 10C). Aadhaar linked: Instant! Get in 3-10 days to bank.
quickAnswers.What is SCSS?: Senior Citizens Savings Scheme: Age 60+, 5 years, ₹30L max, 8.2% interest. Quarterly payout. 80C benefit. Best for retirees!
quickAnswers.Post office schemes 2025?: NSC: 7.7%, PPF: 7.1%, SCSS: 8.2%, KVP: 7.5%, MIS: 7.4%. All govt-backed, safe. Calculate: Post Office Calculator.
quickAnswers.How to transfer shares?: Off-market: DIS slip (demat). On-market: Sell & buy. Gift: Fill gift deed, pay ₹1500 stamp duty. No LTCG on inheritance!
